name: ExtensionMeasure_Group_Population_AggregateMethod
title: R5: Aggregation method for a measure score (e.g. sum, average, median, minimum, maximum, count) (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Measure.group.population.aggregateMethod` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Measure.group.population.aggregateMethod` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Measure.group.population.aggregateMethod` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Measure.group.population.aggregateMethod` has a context of Measure.group.population based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.group.population.aggregateMethod` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Aggregation method for a measure score (e.g. sum, average, median, minimum, maximum, count) (new)
element.comment:
Element `Measure.group.population.aggregateMethod` has a context of Measure.group.population based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Measure`.
Element `Measure.group.population.aggregateMethod` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Aggregation method for a measure score (e.g. sum, average, median, minimum, maximum, count)
element.definition: Specifies which method should be used to aggregate measure observation values. For most scoring types, this is implied by scoring (e.g. a proportion measure counts members of the populations). For continuous variables, however, this information must be specified to ensure correct calculation.
